applied_optimizations.name: Triton kernel fusion
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented two fused Triton kernels: (1) fused_scale_add_clamp_kernel combines scale*2+clamp operations, (2) logsumexp_mish_mult_kernel fuses logsumexp reduction with mish activation. Key fix: combined_factor = scale_factor * 2.0 (not scale_factor + 1.0) because reference does x*scale_factor then x+x.

attempted_but_failed.name: Initial fused kernel with wrong math
Reduced BLOCK_SIZE to 4096
attempted_but_failed.reason: Caused numerical precision issues in logsumexp reduction

notes: Final result: 0.945ms vs 1.020ms baseline, 1.079x speedup, SCORE: 60.8
GEMM dominates runtime at ~93ms (97.9%) - this is the same operation in both implementations
Triton kernels save ~9ms by fusing elementwise ops that take ~11ms in reference (many separate kernels)
Reference has 15+ separate elementwise kernels (add, clamp, exp, log, sum, amax, mish, mul) totaling ~11ms
My implementation has 2 Triton kernels totaling ~2ms for the same operations
Further optimization would require optimizing the GEMM itself (using aiter tuned GEMM or hipBLASLt directly)
ROCm Triton constraints: tl.math.tanh unavailable, must use manual implementation

global_notes: KernelBench Level 2 Problem 22 optimized with Triton on AMD MI355X
Operations: Matmul -> Scale -> Residual Add -> Clamp -> LogSumExp -> Mish
Key insight: x*scale_factor then x+x equals x*(scale_factor*2), not x*(scale_factor+1)
